title: Romanian Old Kingdom

infobox.value: This [Romanian history](/wiki/History_of_Romania "History of Romania")-related article is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Romanian_Old_Kingdom&action=edit).

([Romanian](/wiki/Romanian_language "Romanian language"): *Vechiul Regat* or just *Regat*; [German](/wiki/German_language "German language"): *Regat* or *Altreich*) is a colloquial term referring to the territory covered by the first independent [Romanian](/wiki/Romania "Romania") [nation state](/wiki/Nation_state "Nation state"), which was composed of the [Romanian Principalities](/wiki/Danubian_Principalities "Danubian Principalities"): [Wallachia](/wiki/Wallachia "Wallachia") and [Moldavia](/wiki/Moldavia "Moldavia"). The union of the two principalities was achieved when, under the auspices of the [Treaty of Paris (1856)](/wiki/Treaty_of_Paris_(1856) "Treaty of Paris (1856)"), the [*ad hoc* Divans](/wiki/Ad_hoc_Divans "Ad hoc Divans") of both countries, which were then under [Ottoman Empire](/wiki/Ottoman_Empire "Ottoman Empire") [suzerainty](/wiki/Suzerainty "Suzerainty"), voted for [Alexander Ioan Cuza](/wiki/Alexander_Ioan_Cuza "Alexander Ioan Cuza") as their prince. This process achieved a *[de facto](/wiki/De_facto "De facto")* unification under the name of the [United Principalities of Moldavia and Wallachia](/wiki/United_Principalities_of_Moldavia_and_Wallachia "United Principalities of Moldavia and Wallachia"). The region itself is defined by the result of that political act, followed by the [Romanian War of Independence](/wiki/Romanian_War_of_Independence "Romanian War of Independence"), the inclusion of [Northern Dobruja](/wiki/Northern_Dobruja "Northern Dobruja") and the transfer of the southern part of [Bessarabia](/wiki/Bessarabia "Bessarabia") to the [Russian Empire](/wiki/Russian_Empire "Russian Empire") in 1878, the proclamation of the [Kingdom of Romania](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Romania "Kingdom of Romania") in 1881, and the annexation of [Southern Dobruja](/wiki/Southern_Dobruja "Southern Dobruja") in 1913.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Period in the history of Romania

The **Romanian Old Kingdom** ([Romanian](/wiki/Romanian_language "Romanian language"): *Vechiul Regat* or just *Regat*; [German](/wiki/German_language "German language"): *Regat* or *Altreich*) is a colloquial term referring to the territory covered by the first independent [Romanian](/wiki/Romania "Romania") [nation state](/wiki/Nation_state "Nation state"), which was composed of the [Romanian Principalities](/wiki/Danubian_Principalities "Danubian Principalities"): [Wallachia](/wiki/Wallachia "Wallachia") and [Moldavia](/wiki/Moldavia "Moldavia"). The union of the two principalities was achieved when, under the auspices of the [Treaty of Paris (1856)](/wiki/Treaty_of_Paris_(1856) "Treaty of Paris (1856)"), the [*ad hoc* Divans](/wiki/Ad_hoc_Divans "Ad hoc Divans") of both countries, which were then under [Ottoman Empire](/wiki/Ottoman_Empire "Ottoman Empire") [suzerainty](/wiki/Suzerainty "Suzerainty"), voted for [Alexander Ioan Cuza](/wiki/Alexander_Ioan_Cuza "Alexander Ioan Cuza") as their prince. This process achieved a *[de facto](/wiki/De_facto "De facto")* unification under the name of the [United Principalities of Moldavia and Wallachia](/wiki/United_Principalities_of_Moldavia_and_Wallachia "United Principalities of Moldavia and Wallachia"). The region itself is defined by the result of that political act, followed by the [Romanian War of Independence](/wiki/Romanian_War_of_Independence "Romanian War of Independence"), the inclusion of [Northern Dobruja](/wiki/Northern_Dobruja "Northern Dobruja") and the transfer of the southern part of [Bessarabia](/wiki/Bessarabia "Bessarabia") to the [Russian Empire](/wiki/Russian_Empire "Russian Empire") in 1878, the proclamation of the [Kingdom of Romania](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Romania "Kingdom of Romania") in 1881, and the annexation of [Southern Dobruja](/wiki/Southern_Dobruja "Southern Dobruja") in 1913.

The term came into use after [World War I](/wiki/World_War_I "World War I"), when the Old Kingdom became [Greater Romania](/wiki/Greater_Romania "Greater Romania"), after including [Transylvania](/wiki/Transylvania "Transylvania"), [Banat](/wiki/Banat "Banat"), [Bessarabia](/wiki/Bessarabia "Bessarabia"), and [Bukovina](/wiki/Bukovina "Bukovina"). The term now has mainly a historical relevance and is otherwise used as a common term for all regions in Romania included in both the Old Kingdom and the present borders (Wallachia, Moldavia, and Northern Dobruja).
